title: iOS Chrome mobile player clipped: cap html height at 100svh
description: v8.3.8.8 bumped --player-height 100→200px to address controls clipped behind iOS Chrome's persistent bottom nav. That treats a symptom: the body itself is sized via var(--app-height, 100dvh) and on iOS Chrome both the JS-set --app-height (visualViewport.height) and the 100dvh fallback include the area behind the bottom toolbar, so the player's bottom rows extend behind the toolbar regardless of player height. Fix: cap html height at 100svh via min(). Plan: shell.css:15 + comment update at 213-230, shell.js:887-906 comment, Playwright iOS-Chrome regression test, CHANGELOG v8.3.8.9, dev → QA → Qing real-device proof → release.

title: audiobook-shutdown-saver runs as root but audiobook-save-staging requires audiobooks user (staging never saved on shutdown)
description:
audiobook-shutdown-saver.service has `User=root` (systemd/audiobook-shutdown-saver.service:9) but `/usr/local/bin/audiobook-save-staging` enforces `audiobooks` user and exits 1 if invoked as root:

  `error: audiobook-save-staging must run as the audiobooks user.`
  `current:  root (uid 0)`
  `required: audiobooks`

Result: every shutdown attempt to save tmpfs staging FAILS. Discovered via journalctl during v8.3.9 prod upgrade verification (Audiobook-Manager-hp2 investigation).

Implication: audiobook tmpfs staging is NOT being persisted on clean shutdown despite the unit existing for that purpose. Any in-flight conversions in /tmp/audiobook-staging are lost on reboot.

**Fix options** (need investigation to pick):
1. Change service `User=root` to `User=audiobooks` (correct but may break if save-staging needs root for some path)
2. Change `audiobook-save-staging` to allow root execution (relaxes the permission model)
3. Wrap with `sudo -u audiobooks` in ExecStart (ugly but minimal)

Probably (1) — the script's complaint is that root bypasses the audiobooks:audiobooks ownership model. If the unit runs as audiobooks and the target dirs are owned by audiobooks, no privilege escalation needed.

Verify on dev VM first.
notes: Discovered 2026-04-27 during v8.3.9 prod upgrade verification. journalctl shows the same FAILURE pattern repeating at every upgrade-helper invocation (1983116, 1983663). audiobook-shutdown-saver.service is WantedBy=halt.target reboot.target shutdown.target so it activates only at shutdown — meaning this bug is dormant during normal operation but every reboot loses staged work.

title: upgrade.sh: 'Started ...' phase doesn't start newly-added timers/services (silent gap)
description:
upgrade.sh enables new units (line 956: `systemctl enable`) but the post-upgrade 'Started ...' phase only restarts services that were running before the upgrade. Result: new timers added in a release sit `enabled` but `inactive (dead)` until the next reboot.

Discovered on prod 2026-04-27 16:30 CDT after v8.3.8.14 → v8.3.9 upgrade — both `audiobook-translation-monitor-live.timer` and `audiobook-translation-monitor-sampler.timer` were enabled but never started; `systemctl list-timers` returned 0 matches. Fixed manually via `sudo systemctl start <timer>`.

Same gap affected v8.3.0 `audiobook-stream-translate.service` (memory `feedback_upgrade_consistency_enforcement.md`) — the 'belt-and-suspenders' line at upgrade.sh:963 only enables, doesn't start.

**Fix**: change `systemctl enable "$unit"` to `systemctl enable --now "$unit"` in the standalone_units loop (upgrade.sh:954-959), and the same for line 963 (stream-translate). Also add new units to the post-upgrade restart loop in scripts/audiobook-start so newly-introduced units are actively brought up rather than relying on the next reboot.

**Regression guard**: `/test` Phase V should assert that every timer in /etc/systemd/system/audiobook-*.timer is `active` after a fresh install + upgrade cycle, not just `enabled`.
notes:
Implemented in commits TBD on main:

- upgrade.sh::enable_new_services: replaced bare `systemctl enable` calls (lines 941, 956, 963) with new `_enable_unit_smart` helper that parses [Install] WantedBy= and applies `enable --now` for runtime units, plain `enable` for shutdown-only
- install.sh::do_system_install: same helper inline-duplicated (must stay in sync; both scripts bootstrap before shared shell library is on target)
- install.sh::do_user_install: blanket `enable --now` (only api+web; no shutdown-only units in user-mode set)
- uninstall.sh: reviewed, no change — dynamic-discovery + stop+disable pattern is correct
- CHANGELOG [Unreleased] updated

Two follow-ups filed: shutdown-saver User=root bug (separate); /test Phase V regression guard (claude-test-skill repo).

Verified on real units: helper correctly classifies all 4 monitor timers + stream-translate + scheduler as enable_now, shutdown-saver as enable_only. shellcheck clean.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 4dc3993 — _enable_unit_smart helper applied to upgrade.sh + install.sh (system + user mode); uninstall.sh reviewed, no change needed; CHANGELOG [Unreleased] updated; follow-ups c8l + cf2 filed for the discovered shutdown-saver root-user bug and /test Phase V regression guard.

title: Auto-clear stale upgrade-status lock when helper crashes mid-upgrade
description:
When audiobook-upgrade-helper.service crashes mid-upgrade, /var/lib/audiobooks/.control/upgrade-status is left with running:true and stage:starting_services (or similar mid-flight stage). All subsequent /api/utilities/check-upgrade calls then return HTTP 400 'An operation is already in progress' from library/backend/api_modular/utilities_system.py::_check_not_running, blocking both the in-app upgrade UI and the CLI helper.

Today's incident (2026-04-27): user clicked 'Check for Updates' to upgrade prod from v8.3.8.14 to v8.3.9. UI returned 'Failed to check for updates: An operation is already in progress'. The status file was carrying a 21-hour-old running:true from a 2026-04-26 v8.3.8.13 upgrade that crashed during 'Restoring services after upgrade failure' — the smoke-probe-during-managed-services bug that v8.3.8.14 itself fixed. v8.3.8.14 prevented future occurrences but didn't clean up the existing stale lock.
design:
Mirror the structural pattern of library/translation_monitor/probe.py::reset_stuck_live_claims — same problem class (worker crashes mid-operation leaves a running:true flag), same fix shape (timeout-gated reset).

Approach: in _read_status() (utilities_system.py:95) or as a wrapper called from _check_not_running() (line 178), detect staleness via TWO conditions (defense-in-depth, both required):

1. Status file mtime older than UPGRADE_HELPER_STALE_TIMEOUT_SEC (suggested default: 1800 = 30 min — generous because legitimate upgrades can take 5-10 min, and we don't want to clear a slow-but-healthy upgrade).

2. systemctl is-active audiobook-upgrade-helper.service returns 'inactive' or 'failed'.

When both fire, auto-clear: HELPER_STATUS_FILE.write_text('') and emit a structured log line (or write to a new translation_monitor_events-style audit table). This is the same idempotency pattern used by reset_stuck_live_claims — running it twice with no intervening helper activity is a no-op.

Two-condition gate prevents a false-positive when the helper is genuinely working through a long upgrade (file is being actively written even if the timestamp wraps a checkpoint).
acceptance_criteria:
1. A status file with running:true AND mtime > UPGRADE_HELPER_STALE_TIMEOUT_SEC AND helper service inactive auto-clears on next /api/utilities/check-upgrade call (or on a periodic timer).
2. A status file with running:true AND mtime fresh (<30 min) is left alone (don't break legitimate slow upgrades).
3. A status file with running:true AND helper service active is left alone (don't break legitimate in-progress upgrades).
4. Auto-clear emits a log line so operators can see 'this happened' without it being silent.
5. Test exercises all 4 cases above.
notes:
Filed 2026-04-27 from prod incident response. User on v8.3.8.14 trying to upgrade to v8.3.9 hit blocked-by-stale-lock from yesterday's v8.3.8.13 attempt.

Bundle target: v8.3.10 patch. CHANGELOG entry under ### Added: 'Defensive auto-clear of stale upgrade-helper lock to prevent 'operation in progress' false-blocks after a crashed upgrade.' Equivalent in scope to translation_monitor's claim-reset for streaming_segments.

Threshold rationale: 30 min covers normal upgrades (5-10 min) plus 3x safety margin. Could be tunable via /etc/audiobooks/audiobooks.conf if operators want stricter/looser windows.
close_reason: Fixed in commit bcde439 — _clear_stale_lock_if_safe + _helper_service_active helpers in utilities_system.py with two-condition gate (mtime > 30min AND helper service inactive); fails closed on systemctl probe failure; wired into _check_not_running; 12 new unit tests cover all branches (TestStaleLockAutoClear + TestHelperServiceActive); full utilities_system suite (119 tests) passes — no regression; CHANGELOG [Unreleased] updated under Added.

title: /test Phase V should assert all enabled audiobook-*.timer units are 'active' post-upgrade, not just 'enabled'
description:
Regression guard for Audiobook-Manager-hp2. Phase V currently exercises the install→upgrade→deploy→verify lifecycle, but doesn't differentiate between `is-enabled` and `is-active`. The hp2 bug class (enable without --now) silently passed Phase V because the enable state was correct.

Add an assertion: after a fresh install OR upgrade, every `/etc/systemd/system/audiobook-*.timer` whose unit file is enabled MUST have `systemctl is-active <timer>` returning `active`. Same assertion for any non-shutdown-only `audiobook-*.service` that's enabled.

The shutdown-only carve-out (audiobook-shutdown-saver) is detected the same way the install/upgrade fix detects it: parse `[Install] WantedBy=` from the unit file, treat units WantedBy ONLY halt/reboot/shutdown.target as exempt from the active-after-install assertion.

Lives in claude-test-skill repo: skills/test-phases/phase-V-vm-lifecycle.md (or wherever the lifecycle assertions live).

title: Wire ESLint flat config into web-v2 build
description: Phase 6 added library/web-v2/eslint.config.js (v8.3.9). ESLint 10 won't run cleanly because /hddRaid1/ClaudeCodeProjects/package.json is malformed and npx looks parent-ward. Options: (a) add a local package.json with type=module + devDeps, (b) move config to project root, (c) use eslint --resolve-plugins-relative-to. Pick one and seed CI lint job.
notes:
Bundle target: v8.3.10 patch (public layer only; local layer never ships).

Two layers (per public-vs-local split discussed in /test audit session):

PUBLIC LAYER (ships in v8.3.10 release):
- Add CI lint job in .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs eslint against library/web-v2/eslint.config.js
- CHANGELOG: ### Changed — 'CI now runs ESLint against library/web-v2/js/ using the v8.3.9 flat config'
- This is the part outside operators care about — when they clone the repo and run CI, the lint job runs.

LOCAL LAYER (never ships, stays in this bd issue):
- Resolve npx walking up to /hddRaid1/ClaudeCodeProjects/package.json (malformed parent dir on this dev workstation).
- Three options listed in description; pick one:
  (a) library/web-v2/package.json with type=module + devDeps  → adds node_modules surface but most idiomatic
  (b) move eslint.config.js to project root  → keeps lint visible at top level, slight pollution
  (c) eslint --resolve-plugins-relative-to library/web-v2 in CI  → no source-tree change, CI-only

Filed from /test audit on v8.3.9, Phase 6 follow-up.

title: Direct unit tests for translation_monitor/db.py real-file paths
description: Coverage is 30% via indirect calls. Add direct tests for get_conn() with real sqlite path, fixture cleanup, and the config-fallback branches.
notes:
Bundle target: rides silently in next release — no CHANGELOG entry (Keep a Changelog has no 'Tests' category; coverage gap is internal hygiene, not user-visible).
Scope: tests for library/translation_monitor/db.py lines 35-97 (real-file get_conn, broken-symlink resolution, config-fallback branches). Currently 30% coverage via in-memory fixtures only.
How: add a tmp_path fixture pattern that exercises sqlite3.connect with a real Path, then re-opens the same path, verifies fixture cleanup, and tests the resolve_db_path() config-import fallback (line 35-88). No runtime change; test-only commit. Filed from /test audit on v8.3.9, Phase 4a coverage report.

title: Pin Debian apt versions in Dockerfile
description: Phase 5b found 6 unpinned packages (ffmpeg, mediainfo, jq, curl, libsqlcipher-dev, openssl). Pin to current Trixie versions; revisit on next debian release.
notes:
Bundle target: v8.3.10 patch (or tweak 8.3.9.1 if shipped solo).
CHANGELOG: ### Changed or ### Security — 'Pinned Debian apt versions in Dockerfile for reproducible image builds (ffmpeg, mediainfo, jq, curl, libsqlcipher-dev, openssl)'.
How: in a current Trixie container run `apt-cache madison <pkg>` for each package, capture the exact `<pkg>=<version>` strings, commit. Test `docker buildx build` to verify pins resolve. Filed from /test audit on v8.3.9, Phase 5b finding.
